printers.notes: Launched mid-2026, early bird $379 (regular $499). Open-frame bedslinger sharing the Kobra X motion system and hotend, but uses the external ACE 2 Pro box (4 colors, 8 with a second unit) with dry-while-printing rather than the Kobra X's integrated ACE Gen 2 toolhead.

brands.name: Bambu Lab
brands.notes: Optimized for Bambu printers. RFID tags for auto-detection on AMS. Exceptional out-of-box dryness with sealed packaging. Reusable plastic spools with cheaper cardboard-core refills ($19.99 PLA Basic refill vs $22.99 with spool).

brands.name: Polymaker
brands.notes: Industry-leading material range. PolyLite and PolySonic lines are community favorites. Excellent consistency and tight diameter tolerances. Hygroguard vacuum bags.

brands.name: Prusament
brands.notes: Gold standard for consistency — each spool has a QR code with measured diameter data. Made in EU. New spools ship with the open-source OpenPrintTag NFC tag (since late 2025); Prusa printer reader support rolling out from Q1 2026. PLA launched at $24.99 in 2018, now $29.99. Refills $26.99.

brands.name: Overture
brands.notes: Excellent value. Cardboard spools. Very popular on Amazon. Consistent diameter. Good out-of-box results, especially PLA and PETG.

brands.name: Hatchbox
brands.notes: Long-time community staple. Reliable PLA. US-based support. Limited high-performance materials. Plastic spools. Solid value.

brands.name: eSUN
brands.notes: Massive material selection. One of the oldest filament manufacturers. Budget-friendly. ABS and specialty materials are a strong point. Inconsistent dryness on arrival.

brands.name: Sunlu
brands.notes: World's largest filament producer by volume. Very budget-friendly. Highly variable quality control. Popular for non-critical prints. Dry storage recommended before use.

brands.name: Inland (Micro Center)
brands.notes: Micro Center house brand. Excellent value, especially in-store. Reliable PLA and PETG. Limited specialty materials. Good choice for high-volume printing on a budget.

brands.name: Kexcelled (Tinmorry)
brands.notes: Highly regarded in the community especially for PETG. Very tight diameter tolerances. Excellent print quality for the price.

brands.name: Elegoo Filament
brands.notes: Solid budget option, especially for Elegoo printer owners. Good PLA quality. Now ships open-source RFID (NTAG213 NFC) tagged spools for auto-identification with the Centauri Carbon 2 CANVAS system, with a fully published tag data structure. Refills $13.19. Limited high-performance options.

scoring_methodology.overall: Weighted average of the five sub-scores: print_quality 30%, reliability 25%, speed 15%, ease_of_use 15%, value 15%.
scoring_methodology.speed: Formula-based so identical claims are treated identically: 4.5 + 1.6 * log2(max_speed / 125), minus 0.4 for bedslinger kinematics (a moving bed cannot sustain rated speed on tall or heavy prints), plus documented bonuses for verified acceleration, flow rate, or purge-free toolchanging advantages. Capped at 9.6. Two printers with the same rated speed and the same kinematics receive the same score; any difference is stated in the note.
scoring_methodology.print_quality: Editorial rating informed by published hands-on reviews and community consensus. Rationale per printer in score_notes.
scoring_methodology.reliability: Editorial rating informed by published reviews, brand track record, and failure-prevention hardware. Rationale per printer in score_notes.
scoring_methodology.ease_of_use: Editorial rating informed by setup, calibration automation, and software ecosystem. Rationale per printer in score_notes.
scoring_methodology.value: Editorial rating of capability per dollar against segment peers at current pricing. Rationale per printer in score_notes.
